quote_of_day.text: >>6039 > One muslim girl gets graped > It became international news > Nationwide protest, celebs asking for justice > UC gets raped daily > Nobody gives a damn fuck Who the real winner here
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Send your articles to the East of Indus newspaper
guest_letters.body: By adding the prefix !eastofindus to your post, and writing your title after that, you can send an article through induschan, which will be published on iac and displayed in the next edition of the newspaper, in the morning or the evening! There is no censorship, there is quality control, and there is a 50 word minimum, send your writing!!!
guest_letters.name: India Abstract Culture

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A high pressure system of interracial fixation dominated the morning, with Gorimem️'s musings drawing a steady stream of replies. The discussion was dense and unmoving, like a stubborn anticyclone.

A front of caste-based tension developed in the afternoon, as anons debated the intersection of caste and rape. The air was heavy with argument, the conversation stalled in a cycle of recrimination.

Nearby, a low-pressure system of social anxiety formed around an anon seeking friendship and female interaction. I NEED FRENSS, the opener read, sparking a scattered but intense response.

A brief but intense downpour of praise for a mother of five sparked caste-based bickering, while a distant query about Ivy League attractiveness drifted in on the breeze, drawing a few scattered comments. The atmosphere remained charged, with no clear resolution in sight.

articles.title: Anon Discusses Obsession with Interracial Encounters
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with a single post saying "Gorimem". One respondent expressed puzzlement over another user's apparent obsession with an interracial scenario involving "timmy putri randi" and "shitskin stinky gobar pajeeta", characterizing the fixation as "mind boggling".

This user also mused about the resilience of the Y chromosome to changes, contrasting it with the supposed inferiority of women.

Another anon noted that a particular behavior "feels something a dalit will do", describing it as a "power move" akin to renting a high-quality horse for one's wife to ride.

This user positioned the scenario as a high-IQ, "patrician" taste. One user stated they did not care about the interactions between different groups, characterizing both as "equally bad".

This anon also mentioned noticing people in the USA appearing less "white" and potentially misrepresenting their heritage. A discussion arose around a kink involving watching interracial encounters, with one user asserting they were not gay despite having this kink.

Another user criticized this stance, suggesting it was a form of "faggotry" and implying the user was actually homosexual.

The conversation included various exchanges about race, sexual fantasies, and perceived social hierarchies, with multiple users using derogatory language to refer to different groups.

articles.title: Anons Discuss Caste and Rape
articles.body:
An anon started the thread saying 'Good Night, anons must rest now.' The conversation quickly turned to a discussion about caste and rape.

One anon listed several names, claiming they were OBC, and made a comment about a formulae related to caste dynamics.

Another anon described being a dalit as 'a win win,' saying they look 'dysgenic' and are 'short and are historically stupid.' This anon also made a comment about dalit girls getting raped by OBC.

A respondent countered with information about a specific rape case, mentioning that two of the rapists were a khajuria and a dutta, who are brahmins.

The discussion also involved jokes and insults about various castes, with one anon saying 'pajeets are cockroaches and do not deserve any single human emotions.' Anons also made general statements about rape cases and reactions to them, with one saying 'One muslim girl gets graped > It became international news > Nationwide protest, celebs asking for justice > UC gets raped daily > Nobody gives a damn fuck.' The thread included several exchanges with anons making claims and counter-claims about caste dynamics and reactions to rape cases.

articles.title: Anon Seeks Friendship and Female Interaction
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a simple statement: 'I NEED FRENSS.' One respondent suggested starting with social interactions with women they find unattractive to gain experience.

This anon advised approaching 'uglier foids' to practice talking and to eventually 'looksmaxx' and secure a more attractive partner. Another poster shared their strategy for interacting with women, recommending analyzing the situation and the listener to adjust their approach.

This anon noted that having higher 'izzat points' boosts self-confidence in social interactions.

A different anon expressed frustration with their current social life, stating they have 'lots of female interaction' but find it unfulfilling, comparing it to a 'wet hand on my cock.' This poster longs for a child with a wife instead.

Several anons shared their own struggles with social interactions and making friends. One described humiliating themselves in every social situation and another mentioned their brain going numb in such scenarios.

In a separate line of discussion, an anon mocked another user's claim of having a certain percentile ranking, suggesting it would not lead to significant achievements.

The thread also included a post that joked about a historical figure and a reference to 'Jai Bhim,' which another user responded to with a personal attack.

Anons continued to discuss their personal struggles, with one stating they want a family and envision a future with their children and wife.

This poster expressed a desire to move beyond merely noticing women's physical appearance and instead see them as potential partners and mothers. The conversation remained focused on personal relationships, social interactions, and individual struggles with confidence and friendship.

articles.title: Anon Praises Mother of Five, Sparks Caste-Based Bickering
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying a mother of five looks good and young. Others responded with comments about caste and intermarriage.

One respondent wrote that liking women from other groups isn't an issue, but forced hatred towards certain castes seemed 'faggot coded'.

Another user joked that a hypothetical man from Rohtak wouldn't let his daughter marry someone from another caste. The discussion turned hateful, with some users hurling caste-based slurs and threats.

One user wrote that people of certain castes 'need to be killed'. Another user joked about improving genetics through rape.

articles.title: Anon questions Ivy League attractiveness
articles.body:
Shin expressed surprise at the physical attractiveness of people at an Ivy League school, expecting to find only nerds. He noted that the school is 'chock-full of cute girls'.

Anon replied that Ivy League schools have more prestige outside the US, and that he was attending for a summer program. Another respondent mentioned that many rich and socially prominent individuals attend Ivy League schools.

The conversation turned to college admissions, with Anon discussing the competitiveness of graduate programs and Shin sharing his own experiences with college applications and scholarships.

Some respondents made comments about socializing, relationships, and physical attractiveness, including one who wrote that 'most rich chads go to Ivy Leagues'. The thread also touched on other colleges, including Arizona State University, and the challenges of college admissions.
